food_subgroup: Beef burger with bun and condiments

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product category; no specific code exists for a double-patty deluxe fast-food burger variant. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers hamburger as a generic category. No specific code for double-patty deluxe variant. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 covers hamburger food concept; no specific code for double-patty deluxe variant. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one whole double-patty deluxe burger weighing around 280 g, as typically served at Jollibee fast-food outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Hamburger, double patty with condiments and vegetables (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 21110)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Fast foods, hamburger; double, large patty; with condiments and vegetables' (FDC ID 173960). The Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Double Patty is a comparable double-beef-patty fast-food burger with lettuce, tomato, cheese, and condiments on a toasted bun. Values are scaled to per-100g basis from the USDA reference. Exact Jollibee Singapore nutritional data is not available in any approved primary source; USDA SR Legacy is the closest defensible approved secondary source for this fast-food burger category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Double Patty. GI of 66 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a white-bread-bun hamburger (GI ~66, medium category), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283) which reports GI values for fast-food hamburgers in the medium range (60-70). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 65.5) / 100 ≈ 43.2, where carbohydrate per 280g serving = 23.4 × 2.8 = 65.5g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~430 mg per 100g; ~1204 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this burger as a single serving provides approximately 1204 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive adults.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (66) and high glycemic load per serving (~43)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this burger only occasionally and in reduced portions, as the medium GI of 66 combined with a high glycemic load of approximately 43 per serving can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and avoiding sugary beverages is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~5.1g/100g), trans fat (~0.5g/100g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this burger due to its saturated fat content (~14.3g per serving), presence of trans fat (~1.4g per serving), and high sodium load (~1204 mg per serving), all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and elevated cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Deluxe Jolliburger Double Patty is a fast-food beef burger served at Jollibee, a Filipino-origin quick-service restaurant chain with outlets in Singapore including the Bugis Village location. The burger features two grilled beef patties stacked on a toasted sesame-seed bun, layered with fresh lettuce, sliced tomato, cheese, pickles, mayonnaise, and ketchup. The result is a hearty, savoury burger with a juicy, meaty texture from the double beef patties, balanced by the soft bun and creamy condiments. It is certified Halal, making it accessible to Muslim consumers in Singapore. Nutritionally, the burger is energy-dense at approximately 266 kcal per 100g, with a significant protein contribution (~13.8g/100g) from the double beef patties, moderate-to-high fat content (~13.5g/100g including saturated and trans fats), and a moderate carbohydrate load (~23.4g/100g) primarily from the bun. Sodium content is high (~430mg/100g), raising concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The medium glycemic index (~66) and high glycemic load per serving (~43) make this a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume with caution and in limited portions. This burger is typically consumed as a main meal item at lunch or dinner and is often paired with french fries and a soft drink as part of a combo meal, which would substantially increase the total caloric, sodium, and glycemic load of the meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Jollibee double patty burger?
Is the Jollibee Jolliburger Halal in Singapore?
What is the nutritional value of the Deluxe Jolliburger?
How much protein is in a Jollibee double burger?
Is the Jollibee double burger suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in a Jollibee burger?
What is the GI of a fast-food double beef burger?
Can I eat a Jollibee burger if I have high blood pressure?
meal_context: combo meal with fries and drink

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for the Jollibee Deluxe Jolliburger Double Patty is available in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for a comparable fast-food double-patty hamburger with condiments and vegetables, which is the closest defensible approved source. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using an analogous fast-food hamburger entry. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173960, 'Fast foods, hamburger; double, large patty; with condiments and vegetables') scaled to per-100g basis. Exact Jollibee Singapore product nutritional data is not available in any approved primary source. 2. Serving size of 280g is an estimate based on typical double-patty fast-food burger weights; actual Jollibee product weight may differ. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 4. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for a comparable white-bun hamburger; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific Jollibee product. 5. Trans fat content (~0.5g/100g) is noted from the USDA reference; actual Jollibee product formulation may differ. 6. The food is tagged as Halal per field survey data cuisine tags and Jollibee Singapore's Halal certification status. 7. This record was generated from a field survey data seed record (GD000527) and has been fully processed under Project Veritas v2.
